name: Passover Chocolate Fudge Torte
recipeIngredient: 10 oz. bittersweet or semi-sweet chocolate, chopped
recipeInstructions:
For frosting: Combine all ingredients in a heavy large saucepan. Whisk over medium heat until chocolate melts and mixture is smooth and just begins to bubble, about 8 minutes. Refrigerate until just thick enough to spread, stirring occasionally, about 1 1/2 hours.
Meanwhile, prepare cake: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 C). Line a 15 1/2 x 10 1/2 x 1-inch baking sheet with foil, leaving overhang. Grease foil. Sift together 1/2 cup sugar, cocoa powder, cake meal and potato starch into a medium bowl. Combine egg whites and salt in a large bowl. Using a handheld mixer, beat whites until soft peaks form. Gradually add 1/2 cup sugar; beat until stiff but not dry. Using the same beaters, beat yolks and 1/2 cup sugar in another large bowl until thick, about 2 minutes. Gradually beat in oil, then nondairy creamer. Add dry ingredients; beat just until blended. Fold whites into yolk mixture in three additions.
Spread batter in prepared pan. Bake until a tester inserted into the center comes out dry and the cake feels firm to touch, about 20 minutes. Cool cake on a rack. Freeze cake until firm, about 30 minutes.
Using foil as an aid, lift cake onto a work surface. Cut crosswise into 3 rectangles, each about 5 x 10 inches. Slide a large spatula under one cake rectangle; transfer to a platter. Spread 2/3 cup frosting over. Top with second layer. Spread 2/3 cup frosting over. Top with third layer. Spread a very thin layer of frosting over the top and sides of the cake to coat thinly and anchor crumbs. Refrigerate for 15 minutes to set the thin coat of frosting. Spread remaining frosting decoratively over the cake. Refrigerate until cold, about 4 hours. (Can be made up to 3 days ahead. Cover loosely with foil; keep refrigerated.)
Cover top of cake with chocolate shavings and garnish with mint leaves if desired. Cut crosswise into slices and serve.
(Note: To give credit where credit is due, the recipe is from Bon Appetit, April 1996.)
